summary: PATH hijack via `bin` entry shadowing the `node` runtime
details: package.json declares a `bin` entry literally named `node` pointing to `./shim.js`. On install, npm links this shim into `node_modules/.bin/` (and into the global bin directory on `npm install -g`), which is prepended to PATH during npm script execution. Any subsequent invocation of `node` — by other packages' lifecycle scripts, by build tooling, by CI, or by the developer — runs `shim.js` instead of the real Node runtime, giving the package arbitrary code execution in the installer's environment. `shim.js` confirms hostile intent by writing a proof-of-compromise marker file to `/tmp/.bun-npm-pwned`. The shadowing mechanism is a general-purpose code-execution primitive: the shim can be trivially extended to exfiltrate environment variables, credentials, or source, or to drop further payloads, every time `node` is invoked in the project.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
